title: Logger collector resilience: background health probe, auto-start, gitignore
description: As a developer using @agent-ils/logger, I want the browser logger to not synchronously probe health on every log call when the collector is down, to auto-start the collector with an `open` option, and to have .gitignore auto-created in the log directory.
acceptanceCriteria: browser.ts: ensureReady only checks collectorReady flag, no synchronous probeHealth call
browser.ts: background health probe runs on a 10s interval independently of log calls
browser.ts: when collector is unready, log() returns { ok: true, status: 204 } immediately with zero fetch calls
browser.ts: when postLog fails, markUnready sets collectorReady=false, background probe continues
browser.ts: when open=true, background probing starts immediately on logger creation (not waiting for first log call)
browser.ts: when open=true and running in Node env, spawn the Go collector binary as child process
index.ts: startHttpLogServer creates .gitignore with `*` in the log directory after mkdir
Go collector server.go: create .gitignore in log directory on startup
logger.instructions.md updated to reflect new behavior
notes: User explicitly requested these 3 features. The health check fix is the most critical - current behavior causes CONNECTION_REFUSED on every log call when collector is down.
